reviews.title: Missing item
reviews.text:
stayed at your hotel and realised that a watch was accidentally left behind in the room. We have checked and the last known location of the watch shows it is still at the hotel. 
Too bad we can get the watch back. So have to lock the watch  online at the end.
reviews.response:
Dear Valued Guest,

Thank you for leaving us your feedback. We regret to note that your overall experience did not quite live up to your expectations, and we sincerely apologize for the inconvenience this had caused. We would greatly appreciate any additional feedback you can provide to help us understand more regarding the lost watch mentioned. Would appreciate if you could provide us your contact number and email address for us to communicate better.

Warm Regards,

Hotel Management

reviews.text: Not nice customer service,  not good room. Not good food
reviews.response:
Dear Mr Ahmad,

Thank you for taking the time to complete our on-line guest survey after your recent stay at Hilton Garden Inn Kuala Lumpur JTAR. Your feedback is particularly valuable to us.

I was concerned that from your feedback and ratings generally suggests that our performance on this occasion was not outstanding.  Rest assured your feedback has been duly noted and I have addressed your concerns with my management team to ensure we focus on lifting our service levels and attention to detail in the areas you have highlighted.
 
I do apologise that we did not meet all your expectations in all areas on this occasion.  I would however encourage you to treat this as an exception rather than a reflection of the hotel. The Team and I look forward to the opportunity of welcoming you back soon.

Kind Regards,
Mohan Raj Thanapal,
Hotel Manager
Hilton Garden Inn Kuala Lumpur, Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman

reviews.title: Does not meet Hilton Name
reviews.text: This is extremely  low rated hotel and terrible side of KL. Rats on the street drug addicts lined in the street. Motorcycles  running on footpaths full speed. No place to park vehicles for safe check in or check out. Ordered room service  with it coming  on a tray which may not have been cleaned  over a month. Hot water issues with shut Downs taking place for repairs. Access key lock for room door malfunctioning. 80% of staff are on trainees. The only outstanding 2 staff the hotel security one who's name is Rajesh, there is no porter or bell boy service, however the security guards go over and beyond to help with baggage. Seems like management  needs to pay more attention to  operations rather than bookings. Overall was a horrible stay the hotel is very  focused  on large tour bookings  which have guests that are nocturnal which slam doors all night long and with the biscuit walls you can hear everything. If your on the 3rd floor  the monorail passengers  can see inside the room. I have been hiltons before nothing comes close to this place. I would give a negative  star if I could. Only shiny staff where the security  guards  that helped with baggage  and engaged with guest. Senior management  needs to be gone from this hotel  as the place is not managed it is a jungle.

reviews.title: Don’t go for breakfast buffet
reviews.text:
We had booked 3 rooms with twin tower view. We reached to hotel at around 11 am but since the check in time is 3:00 pm we didn’t get any room till 1 pm.  We came with baby and requested to provide temporary room but we had to wait 2 hrs with baby after a long journey. 

The rooms were awesome with twin tower view from window. It also has all the amenities the room should have. Location is also nice with 7-11 and KK Mary situated nearby.

Regarding the breakfast buffet, it was pathetic as a Indian. It was just a waste of money to opt for breakfast buffet. The only thing in the buffet you will like is bread and butter with coffee.

They generally don’t have bellboy availability so you may have to carry your luggage to and from room.

reviews.text: This is the first time i am staying this hotel. I had the bad experience to stay in this hotel. They force u to use the valet parking services and pass your car key to them while u park yr car inside property in betweens I saw them drive yr car like Fast and furious. They are no right to hold our car key. This is the bad system.When the time we don't pass key to the manager he challenge us to call agoda. Luckily agoda officers always be there to support us. In the end luckily we have a good reception willing to help us. Just want to complain and remind to u Hilton Garden Inn management. U should control yr manager to not say the no manner words. And the valet service u provide is a very bad decision. I just can say this is the last time i am staying this hotel. Gentle reminder to anyone who drive to this hotel. Pls consider other hotel. This is not yr right choice. U will regret and face the same issue like me.

Dear Tan,

Thank you for making Hilton Garden Inn KL your hotel of choice during your most recent trip. Please do reach out to us directly since we would like to deliver you an improved experience on your next stay. We look forward to welcoming you again soon.

Kind regards,
Mohan Raj Thanapal
Hotel Manager

reviews.text:
The hotel services require significant improvement in punctuality and attentiveness. Staff members often appear disengaged and lack genuine concern for their clients’ needs. Guests are left in situations where they must repeatedly ask for guidance or assistance, rather than receiving proactive support.  

There is a noticeable absence of initiative from the staff, which creates an impression of indifference and poor hospitality standards. Instead of anticipating guest requirements or offering helpful suggestions, the team tends to wait until issues are raised, resulting in delays and frustration.  

For a hotel experience to be truly satisfactory, staff should demonstrate awareness, empathy, and a willingness to go beyond basic duties. Proactive service such as offering directions, anticipating needs, and ensuring timely responses, would greatly enhance the guest experience. Without these qualities, the overall atmosphere feels transactional rather than welcoming, which undermines the essence of hospitality.

reviews.title: No more next time with this hotel
reviews.text:
Usually I stay frequently at Ibis, but this time I wanna try new hotel because this look class and comfy from the picture shown. I heard that this hotel is quite famous and high class. 

Things that makes me feel regret choosing this hotel:
1. Parking issue: This hotel only has limited parking lot and only allowed valet help you to park your car. Anyone who drive, this hotel definitely not a good option. The front desk tell me to park at public parking about a few hundreds meter away from the hotel. Then you have to walk back to the hotel again. Plus it is so unsecure to park at a distance away and need to pay for the parking fee extra.

2. No ramp for you to slide your luggage: From main entrance to the lobby and from the lobby to take the lift are staircases. no ramp to slide your luggage. (Even they do have the electronic slide for OKU, but normal person you have to carry your luggage twice to get the lift.

3. Room different from what I booked in Agoda: I booked Queen Room with View. When i step in the room, oh god, it's a room right beside the lift (but no sound of lift moving up and down heard when sleep in the room). I booked a room with window and a long bench/sofa, but there is nothing. The room is so small.

4. Aircond cannot be controlled: When we feel cold, we turn off the aircond but the cold air still flow into the room which is useless to use the remote.

5. Soundproof: Biggest noise is from the toilet. Next room turn on tv and singing can be heard from the toilet. Like it is so close. Cannot sleep well after 8am if person next to the room is wake up.

6. The shower heater is so unstable: It turn cold and hot in few seconds repeatly. Means you will get hot for 3 seconds then warm 3 seconds then hot 3 seconds then warm 3 seconds until you finish bath.

7. Shower water pressure low: Not very low, but like your house shower pressure, not like what a hotel should have.
reviews.response:
Dear Lim,

Thank you for making Hilton Garden Inn KL as your choice of accommodation during your most recent trip. However, we regret noticing that you were not able to experience the warmth of our hospitality to the fullest. please accept our sincere apologies for all the inconvenience you had experienced on this occasion. Please do reach out to us directly as we would like to deliver you an improved experience on your next stay. We would greatly appreciate another opportunity to welcome you back next time, ensuring a much better and improved experience for you.

Kind regards,
Mohan Raj Thanapal
Hotel Manager

First room had strong air irritant odor, like smoke, maybe vape, but they said no smoke smell found, but quickly changed room easily. However, in the new clean room, all the soaps were empty on arrival, had to delay shower after eight hours of travel. During the stay, the towels weren’t replaced so had to wait nearly an hour after informing the front desk to shower after busy work into the evening out. One time and concerningly, the elevator had problem and abruptly shut down stopped for a couple minutes while going down. Front desk was apathetic and treated my raised concerns which should not have even been an issue as if I was yet another overly demanding unpleasant guest. To top it all off, the gym had no air conditioning or fans, with inadequate room so you can on the pull up bar machine hit your head onto the ceiling. Better think twice, the value might not live up to reasonable expectations.

It’s fine for a short overnight stay, but I wouldn’t recommend it for a longer visit. If you’re arriving by car, valet parking is available, but the directions and parking guidance were unclear, which made the experience quite inconvenient.

Our room initially smelled of cigarettes and the carpet didn’t feel clean. The staff did upgrade our room, which we appreciated. However, as we had traveled quite a distance, we were hoping for a smooth check-in experience without needing to raise any concerns.

I understand the pricing, but other hotels at a similar rate offer a better overall experience, so there’s definitely room for improvement. I would consider coming back if these issues are addressed.
reviews.response:
Dear Nurul,

Thank you for taking the time to share your detailed feedback with us. We are pleased to hear that our team was able to assist with a room upgrade and that you appreciated their effort.

We sincerely apologise for the inconvenience you experienced upon arrival, particularly with the valet parking guidance and the initial condition of your room. This is certainly not the seamless experience we aim to provide, and your comments regarding cleanliness and room preparation have been shared with the relevant teams for immediate review.

We appreciate your honest comparison and feedback on overall value. Your comments are very important to us as we continuously look for ways to improve both our service and guest experience.

Thank you once again for your feedback. We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back in the future for a much-improved experience.

Yours Sincerely,
Hilton Garden Inn Kuala Lumpur Management Team

reviews.title: Misled by hilton brand
reviews.text:
- check in was ok
- rooms quite small, probably our mistake should have checked the size before booking. When we added an extra cot there was no space.
- bathroom had kind of a smell.. 
- rooftop garden and swimming pool is on south tower. No connection from north. 
- we had to go through main road footpath to go to south tower for swimming.  A small pool. Changing room was closed as there were cleaning going on
- roof top restaurant.  Slow service.. no one checking on guests.  Took time to get attention and had to walk over to counter to order a drink.
- breakfast:  very poor. Do not even try the Indian options - pathetic. Even a novice if they have seen you tube videos can prepare better. 
- check out: peak time at 12, there is only 1 person at the check out counter.
- no parking near the hotel entrance.  
- 7 steps from check in to lift lobby.  While there is an access option,  not convenient if you are having someone aged  or will need assistance in taking the steps

I am sure I won't choose a hilton garden inn again for stay..

reviews.title: There are many hotel choices in KL, avoid this hotel
reviews.text: I rarely give reviews about staying experiences. But this time it has to be. I got an accidental insect bite on my right upper arm and wrist on the first day of my stay. Felt it after I showered and had breakfast at the hotel. Reported at the first FO, waited 10 minutes before the cleaning staff arrived, she didn't find any insects on the bed then took pictures of the lumps on several points of my hand which were itchy and I asked for a change of room. And after 10-15 minutes there was still no information looked for insects on the bed myself, found and videoed it. Then go down myself to the second FO  and asked for a change of room. A surprise, this hotel didn't have a clinic or any medicine to treat me. The FO officer told me to take a Grab by myself and go to the nearest clinic. I actually didn't have a problem with it until Mr Calvin, a FO Manager, said by Whatsapp that previous guests didn't have the same problem as me. I don't care about previous guests, I got bitten and there are bugs in your hotel right now. I have stayed in dozens of hotels over the years and have never had an insect bite except at your hotel. And you said would investigate this problem, but during the 3 nights I stayed and checkout there was no further information from the results of your investigation. This is very disappointing. Secondly problems, in all Hilton hotel chains, I have stayed at Conrad Bali, Doubletree Jakarta, Hilton Garden Inn Cengkareng, and Surabaya, breakfast at the branch here is below standard. For 3 nights breakfast, you usually serve 2 types of porridge every day, no croissants, tasteless tea tarik, tasteless omelettes, daily roti prata, tasteless vermicelli with just onions as a side dish. It's really sad and I really don't recommend my friends to stay here.

reviews.title: Parking is a problem
reviews.text:
If you are driving I am really not recommend you to stay this hotel. They only have vallet parking which cost you RM15 per car per night and the basement parking is at the back of the hotel in a very small alley where a lot of shops there. Many trucks and cars stop there to load / unload stocks to cause the road couldn't go through. The vallet is not professional at all, you have to go front desk to ask them to go to the back to open the gate for you. I stop my car there for 15mins but nobody come to open for me, there is no intercom for you to call the vallet too. I call the hotel but nobody pick up the call. End up my wife have to walk to front desk to seek for help but the front desk just ask us to wait. 

Worst part is the vallet even issue the parking card with wrong information. I provide 2 cars number and the wrote down correctly on the receipt but when issue the parking card he written wrongly. 

DON'T LOST THE CARD, THEY ARE WAITING TO CHARGE YOU RM20. During check out I go to the vallet counter to show them my parking card and ask them to open the gate for me, then they follow me to the back and request the card from me. I suddenly couldn't found the card and tell him I already show you just now right? He said he need the card, "If don't have just pay the card lost only la"

The hotel rooms are ok and breakfast are acceptable too.

reviews.title: Not even sure if they are part of Hilton group
reviews.text:
We stayed here for 2 nights hoping to get a decent rest after a long trip around Asia. We checked in close to midnight, but unfortunately, the staff didn’t acknowledge us at all—no greeting, no “Do you have any questions?”—nothing. I overheard the staff explaining things like breakfast to the guests before us, but we weren’t given any information.

Having worked in hospitality myself, I’m usually not too fussy, but the service really fell short. The swimming pool and bar aren’t even on-site; they seem to be located next door at the Hilton Garden South.

To top it off, the neighbouring room was extremely noisy after 10:30pm, which made it hard to get the rest we were hoping for. We left our bags to another hotel before heading to our next destination (we don’t to travel with too much, which—even though it’s not a Hilton—offered much better service even before we checked in many days before. 

Just sharing my personal experience for anyone considering this place.

The hotel room we had booked was Queen room with Petronas Twin Tower view, the room had sitting area near windows where you can enjoy the view, with a small dining table, the room size was decent not too big, bathroom was big.
Let’s talk about REAL part now, the hotel is located in a very poor area, I personally did not like the locality, also the pool is not in the same building, you have to go in the building beside which is not interconnected so you have to exit and reenter the other building for the pool, also there were no bellhops and there is a staircase where you have to drag your heavy luggage by yourself which was very disappointing, furthermore the breakfast menu is also not great, I read in earlier reviews that there is variety of dishes but in reality there is no variety and all items were cold and not kept on warmer. Also the receptionist are very rude.

I might not consider here for next visit.

the service i asked for, spoon the first day.

overall, i do not recommend this hotel for stay.

reviews.title: Slow service
reviews.text:
I booked 3 rooms. From What I understand is that every hotel their check in time is either 2 or 3 pm. I arrived at 3:30 pm and my room wasn’t ready.

It was already a long journey from Singapore with two toddlers on board. The check in process was already very slow. I don’t understand what is the purpose of them making a copy of all of our passport. She said it’s for the tourist tax. When Before that I already gave my mum’s Malaysian IC (she’s a Malaysian). 

Then I still have to wait for my rooms. After about 10mins then I only get 1 of my rooms. I already told them that I do not need a connecting room. But they insist and I end up waiting till 5 pm for the other two rooms. They also didn’t even bother to check on me or update me whether the room is ready or not. 

And one more thing, I request for housekeeping. But when I came back, the room was incomplete. They did not top up the coffee and tea.. 

Fyi, I stayed here before. It was good. That’s why I came back but now I am truly disappointed.

reviews.title: Good, but could be a lot better for the price spent.
reviews.positives:
Courteous, hospitable, & professional staff.
The location is close to everything.
The security is very good; you need to use your room card to use the elevator lift going up (no need for it going down).
reviews.negatives:
Very thin walls; you can hear your neighbors speaking, laughing, & moving very loud & clear.
The door is not sealed; light & sound clearly seep through. Not recommended if you’re a very light sleeper.

Some of the bathrooms have issues & need updating:
The first room I booked had a broken bidet/sprayer where the hose was leaking & would spray all over the place. The water also had no drain near the toilet, so the water had nowhere to go except the shower drain, so the whole bathroom becomes flooded with water as it reaches the drain. This was not the case in the second room I booked which was a queen-size bed room.

I think $30-40/night is a suitable price for their single room. $40-50/night for their queen-size bed rooms. Anything over that is somewhat overpriced, as I see it.

Rest assured your feedback has been duly noted and I have addressed your concerns with my management team to ensure we focus on lifting our service levels and attention to detail.

As noted at our website and through all other booking channels, car parking is not a room rate inclusion at this hotel. As we do not own the car parking facility, we opt to pass the small discounted daily charge onto those guests that do bring a vehicle.
 I do apologise that we did not meet all your expectations in all areas on this occasion.  I hope this will not impact your impression towards the Hotel and we look forward to the opportunity of welcoming you back soon.

Kind regards,
Hilton Garden Inn Kuala Lumpur, JTAR

reviews.title: You can consider only if you’re not driving.
reviews.text:
The hotel is situated in the midst of a busy street. Street food nearby. But the North and South buildings are next to each other, so if you don’t look properly, you might miss it. 

Room and bed is comfortable. Stayed at the 16th floor with the twin tower view.

Allowed late check out at 1pm. This is helpful. 

Housekeeping was good. Prompt and helpful with the extra floor mat and bottled water. Thank u! 

CONS:

Toilet is noisy with non stop noise from the pipes above I assume. If you don’t close the toilet door, the noise is constantly annoying.  

Shower feature is basic with a glass divider but water will still manage to seep out to the dry area. We had to change the floor mat twice just for one shower. Floor mat is fully soaked when the water leaks out. 

IF YOU DRIVE, please reconsider. There is parking in the hotel, BUT you have to park at the main roadside (bad jam at any given time of the day), provided you can find a way to the curb side, and then alight, go to the valet counter, and wait for them (if they are busy and not around) to help you drive your car to the parking spaces in the basement.

If your car is low like ours, be prepared to have it scratched cos the slope is steep. The valet will keep your key for you. We don’t feel safe with this idea and spoke to the front desk about it, and nobody can tell us who’s going to be responsible if the car was damaged on the way to the carpark or if anything happens to the car since our key is with the valet. Not very assuring. 

We drove ourself into the carpark, but the jam is very very bad as all the cars are going to turn into the same lane. The carpark is at a very small alley, which is filled with trash. Once you open your car door, the stench comes! Terrible. And it’s dark at night too. Not safe for ladies I would say. 

Other than that, both hotel and valet personnel are considering nice enough. Appreciate it. 

So think twice if you’re gonna drive to this hotel! Stressful experience honestly.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Hotel location is quite close to LRT station however located next to a small houses near a market. For our hotel room view was the neighborhood houses rear side with their showering places, garbage collections and mixes run all the way. Even though the view of twin towers are visible we have barely opened our curtains since people are moving half naked or women showering is the view you get. Rooms are not spacious enough and our room AC was not working properly, bathroom shower area glass is having a space between the follow and glass which makes water come out every time we shower. Had to kept a towel between the floor and the glass to absorb water. Breakfast buffet was very limited and refill was barely done. Variety was limited and at the end of day 4 or 5 we were tired of eating the same thing every morning. Rooms are not sound proof and people who were next to our room was having gatherings in their room till early morning which disturbed our sleep. Pool and roof top bar both located at the next hotel which is 1 building away. Basically you have to go out from your hotel to Hilton South to access both pool and roof top bar. When it comes to staff they are excellent. From security officers to receptionists to the room service staff they all provide an excellent service to all guests.

reviews.title: The Reviews online are untenable
reviews.text:
FOA for those who wonder, the Inn's "North" & "South" branches are just 100m from each other. The south apparently has better facilities - including a rooftop bar and a small swimming pool. We stay in the "North" and were told we can use the pool at the "south".
Your mileage might vary but the room we had made me consider paying for another hotel room. We had a 1st floor room. Noise from motorbikes can be heard at night. The bed was tiring and we didn't have a good rest. While the toilet was not dirty, the sink didn't drain well.

Location wise, there are not many options for good food, some interesting local street food can be found (if your stomach is up to the challenge) around GM Plaza (around 300m walk) and the street-side stalls outside the wet market can be interesting for tourists to see the older side of KL. The wet market is rat-infested though, so it might not be for everyone. There are still some signs of the old Malay kampung baru beyond the wet market and can provide interesting views of the twin towers with old traditional kampung houses in the foreground.

Transport-wise, public transports require a fair bit of walking. Grab and taxi can stop at the kerbside right in front of the hotel.

Overall, this is not a hotel we would choose to stay in again, even if we want to stay in the Chow Kit area again. There are better hotels around the same price range in the area.
